entry.title: How do we migrate Open Accruals into a Condition Contract?
entry.content:
Hi all,

How do we migrate Open Accruals in a Condition Contract?

We are doing an implementation in S/4HANA and would like the open accruals to be migrated into the Condition Contract so that the subsequent Final Settlement will consider these amounts.

We do not want to migrate business volumes from legacy system as the number of lines to be created then goes to a very high volume. Is there any other way to do it?

In case the only option is to migrate business volumes, do we have any program or standard way to load it? We do not want to create billing documents as that would again mean creating more upfront documents like sales orders etc.

entry.title: Troubleshooting guide for issues when using API API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog I will outline the steps you can follow to troubleshoot issues when calling the API API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV and common questions and errors that can arise from it use.

I work in Technical Support for S/4HANA Cloud and help Customers with issues involving our API and see recurring themes and though it was best to create a guide customer can follow in how to maintain the correct payload / communication user setup so they can upload and download attachments without contacting SAP Support. 

 

This guide will not cover issues involving Harmonized Document Management (HDM)  

 

When investigating issue involving the API API_CV_ATTACHMENT the points I check are 

  • Check Communication Scenario assigned is correct.  
  • Check BusinessObjectTypeName used in the payload is correct. 
  • Check LinkedSAPObjectKey is in the right format. 

 

Below I will outline different scenarios to illustrate how I perform these checks. 

 

Scenario 1: Check Communication Scenario assigned is correct.  

What Communication Scenario do I need to assign for adding or download attachments from an object like Sales Order, Purchase Order, Product? 

The First step to answer this question is to check the SAP API Hub for our API  API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV and section Communication Scenarios . Here you can see all the communication scenarios that use our Attachment API. 

 

It might be not clear how to make the association between the communication scenario and the object to which you want add or retrieve an attachment so you take a communication scenario, like Sales Order Integration (SAP_COM_0109) that you suspect can be used for Attachments with Sales Orders and search the Help Portal (Documentation) resource for S/4HANA Cloud. 

 

In the search results you will find entries for the communication scenario and the API used to update / create the master object itself like API_SALES_ORDER_SRV for Sales 

 

If you click on these links and navigate to the application specific documentation it should outline how to add, read, or delete attached using our Attachment API 

 

For the property BusinessObjectTypeName you can see the value BUS2032 (sales order) is shown.  

This confirms that to use the API API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV for Sales Order attachments the communication scenario SAP_COM_0109 needs to be assigned.  

 

Scenario 2: Check BusinessObjectTypeName used in the payload is correct. 

Why do I get the authorization error like ‘User has no authorization for operation XX on object’ When  using the API API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV and Entity GetAllOriginals? 

 

The reason is usually to the wrong or missing assignment of Communication Scenario to a communication user or the parameter LinkedSAPObjectKey is in the wrong format. 

 

Example: 

The API API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV is called with a Communication User that has SAP_COM_0008 assigned. 

In Postman a GET Request using the Communication User and payload below is used 

/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/GetAllOriginals?LinkedSAPObjectKey='5123456782024'&BusinessObjectTypeName='BUS2081' 

Postman returns the Response: 

User has no authorization for operation XX on object BUS2081  

 

Troubleshooting steps: 

The first step is to clarify what object you want to retrieve the attachment meta information from. 

The example involves the BusinessObjectTypeName 'BUS2081' 

Using the steps provided in Scenario 1, I search the Help Portal (Documentation) resource for S/4HANA Cloud for 'BUS2081' to find the application specific documentation and in the search results I find “Attachments within Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0057” 

 

When navigating to this documentation I can see the following information 

Within the communication scenario SAP_COM_0057 the following services are available: 

  • Supplier Invoice – Create (A2X) 
  • Supplier Invoice – Create, Read, Release, Reverse 
  • Attachment - Create, Read, Rename, Delete 

 

BusinessObjectTypeName: Defines the business object for the supplier invoice BUS2081 (supplier invoice) 

LinkedSAPObjectKey: Enter the key without space between the numbers for the invoice and the fiscal year. <invoice number><fiscal year> 

 

In the example I want to retrieve attachment meta information from a supplier invoice so I can see 

I’m using the correct BusinessObjectTynameName BUS2081 in my payload. 

The LinkedSAPObjectKey I am supplying in the payload '5123456782024' is in the correct format. 

(Invoice Number 51234567 and Fiscal year 2024 concatenated without spaces) 

The last check then is the communication scenario I have assigned to my communication user. I have assigned SAP_COM_0008 and the Documentation requires SAP_COM_0057 to be able to use BusinessObjectTypeName BUS2081. 

This appears to be the reason the authorization error ‘User has no authorization for operation XX on object BUS2081 ‘is raised. 

If I assign SAP_COM_0057 to my communication user, the authorization error will not occur. 

 

Scenario 3: Check LinkedSAPObjectKey is in the right format. 

Why do I get the authorization error like ‘You have No Authorization for Attachment’ when adding an  attachment using API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV and Entity AttachmentContentSet? 

The same steps outlined in Scenario 2 apply here as well. It can be due to either the correct  

Communication Scenario is not assigned or the parameter LinkedSAPObjectKey in the payload is in the wrong format. 

 

Example : 

 

The API API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV is called with a Communication User that has SAP_COM_0002 assigned. 

In Postman a POST Request using the Communication User, URL 

/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/AttachmentContentSet and payload below is used 

Postman returns the Response: "You have No Authorization for Attachment”. 

Troubleshooting steps: 

As stated previously, the initial step is to clarify that I have the correct communication scenario assigned and LinkedSAPObjectKey is in the correct format. 

I search the Help Portal (Documentation) resource for S/4HANA Cloud with SAP_COM_0002 to find the master data API for updating the object involved. 

 

 

I can see in the results the Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0002 is used in Finance. 

When navigating to the application specific document I can see SAP_COM_0002 is used for creating / updating and reading from Journal Entries. 

In the example payload, I want to add an attachment (PDF document ) to a Journal Entry so the Communication Scenario  that I’m using  (SAP_COM_0002 ) appears to be correct 

Next, I need to verify I’m using the right BusinessObjectTypeName 

In my example I have specified BusinessObjectTypeName as BKPF 

I search the Help Portal (Documentation) resource for S/4HANA Cloud and no application specific document is available For SAP_COM_0002 and BusinessObjectTypeName  BKPF .  

I search the Support Launchpad and find the KBA  Journal Entry Attachments not returned when using the API API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV which confirms BKPF is the correct BusinessObjectTypeName  is use. 

We have verified the correct communication scenario to use and BusinessObjectTypeName so next step is to ensure the LinkedSAPObjectKey I have maintained is in the correct format. 

In our example we have supplied LinkedSAPObjectKey  as  is 1900000001 ( The Journal Entry ) 

The KBA (Journal Entry Attachments not returned when using the API API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV) outlines the Format of the LinkedSAPObjectKey when using BusinessObjectTypeName BKPF 

“For BusinessObjectTypeName BKPF should be should be built by combination of key fields which is company, document number and year. 

Please take care of the document number.  The document number field has length 10. if you document number length is less than 10, please add leading zero.” 

If I apply this logic to my LinkedSAPObjectKey the result is 100019000000012024  

If I try again using a Post request using the same communication scenario and BusinessObjectTypeName but this time with LinkedSAPObjectKey as 100019000000012024 , the document should be upload and no error ‘You have No Authorization for Attachment’ raised. 

 

Useful KBA’s and Blogs

'User has no authorization for operation 03 on object BUS2037' when calling API API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV , Entity GetAllOriginals 

Error 'User has no authorization for operation 03 on object VBRK_BD' when calling API API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV, Entity GetAllOriginals   

Message 'The URL you entered is not configured as a trusted site' when attaching document in an App or using API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV    

Cloud Integration (SAP CPI) - API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV (OData API for attachments)

Maintain Assignment of Document Info Record to Maintenance Task Lists via API, OAuth and Postman 

Create a pdf attachment using the Attachments API in S/4HANA Cloud

 Access a sales order document through an external system using the Attachments API 

ODATA API ‘Attachments’ Service in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

found the process to delete the records from staging table. For example to delete the data from staging table go to the initial project screen 

Click on the tables number. It will take you to the next screen where it will show all the records as below. Select all records or the rows to be deleted and press delete records on top of the rows (highlighted in green) to delete the records.

You can check the successful deletion through SE16 for the staging table. 

Note: the staging table name is also shown on the same screen

entry.title: A working SPML request to SAP UME
entry.content:
Hello

Could anyone share a working SPML request for SAP UME to retrieve a list of all users?
I've tried different variants in Postman, but without success—either I get an empty response or an error like:

<SOAP-ENV:Envelope xmlns:SOAP-ENV="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">
	<SOAP-ENV:Header/>
	<SOAP-ENV:Body>
		<searchResponse error="urn:oasis:names:tc:SPML:1:0#customError" result="urn:oasis:names:tc:SPML:1:0#failure">
			<errorMessage>The HTTP request is not valid. For details see SAP Security Note 1631354.</errorMessage>
		</searchResponse>
	</SOAP-ENV:Body>
</SOAP-ENV:Envelope>

I tried the following URLs:

http://<host>:<port>/spml/spmlservice
http://<host>:<port>/spml/provisioning

My ultimate goal is to connect to SAP UME from an external Java program (not hosted on NetWeaver AS Java) and retrieve various types of information from SAP UME, including the user list.

entry.title: SuccessFactors Story Reports - Aggregation problem calculated measure
entry.content:
I have the following requirement:

If a recruiting job posting is published externally and internally per job requisition, then it should count as external. If a recruiting posting is only published externally, then it should count as external, if internally, then as internal. All should be aggregated by Job Requ ID. My problem: As the function SUM or Aggregation is not available in calculated measure, the only way to build a total sum is to use GrandTotal. Here I face the problem it is building the total over all job requ´s and not group it by / aggregate it also here by job req. Is there a workaround to fulfill the requirement with the available functions in story reports?

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay Budget Calculation
entry.content:
Hi @Olga_Skarla  please check and explore possible configuration options using this KBA : https://me.sap.com/notes/2323284

I have never tried to build something like this and I am still wrapping my head on your explanations. If you use the Result budget then the Total will be the sum of individual goal payouts (factored by the section weight which in your case should be at 100% since you are not using business or team sections).

Why would you want the Total shown in the budget to not be aligned with the sum of all calculated payouts ?

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: How to refresh the Feature Traceability view (and oth...
entry.content:
Hi, Bruno,

That is my experience as well -> the Feature Traceability report does not have an embedded 'Refresh' icon. 

If I switch the search field to trigger a new set of results (e.g. set a different status that is presently displayed) and allow it to generate the results and afterwards, revert to the prior criteria, the status values do update to the current values (if they have changed between the earlier page generation and the re-launch of the query).

To me, the Browser page's refresh icon seems the most convenient, at least in my use.

As I mentioned in your earlier question, feel free to submit an ideas/requests for changes in the Influencer Campaign:

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501
 - SAP Cloud ALM for Operations https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3525

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Re: CAP Service returns 503 service unavailable when run...
entry.content: This blog post is already old, but what helped me with the exact 503 message after deployment to BTP CF, even with App Router and correct setup was, that it looks for the destination not on subaccount upper level but within the CF Space and the service instance itself. So you have to specify the explicite destination for destination service instance in the corresponding UI. Afterwards, it can connect via destination and cloud connector.

entry.title: Deployment Scheduling in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Introduction

Check out the new Deployment Scheduler in SAP Cloud ALM! 

For those collaborating with distributed development teams and adhering to a release-driven strategy, the ability to schedule deployments offers a substantial advantage. With the new Deployment Scheduler effortlessly orchestrate status-dependent deployments for SAP NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP (version 7.40 or higher), SAP S/4HANA on-premise, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Simply select your desired features, and the corresponding transport set will be imported as "Import All" into the designated target tenant.

To access the Deployment app you need to have the Deployment Manager, Project Administrator, or Global Administrator role.

How exactly does this process function, and what preparation steps are necessary to utilize it effectively?

Preparation

The deployment schedule is based on one deployment plan and the assigned system groups. All tenants of system roles Quality Assurance, Pre-production and Production assigned to the corresponding system groups can be selected as target tenants for deployment scheduling.

Note: In case your target tenant (client) is not available in LMS we recommend to activate the use case "Health Monitoring" in /SDF/ALM_SETUP of the respective client (learn more about Health Monitoring) or add the client manually in LMS.

Learn more about deployment plans and system groups.

Deployment Plan:

System Group:

In the example above the target tenants O12.100 and O13.100 can be selected in the Deployment Scheduler.

Since one deployment plan can be assigned to several projects the deployment scheduler is considering features and transports of several projects running in parallel. If you want to create a deployment schedule for a particular project you must create one particular deployment plan for this project.

Note:

  • If a deployment plan is assigned to at least one private project you're not authorized to access, you can't schedule a deployment plan. In this case, ask your responsible Project Lead to grant you access to all the private projects or request the Project Administrator or Global Administrator role.

  • Changing private project authorizations after you created a schedule doesn't have any effects on the deployment schedule.

Caution: When you assign a deployment plan to another project after you've created a schedule, all features created in this newly assigned project and the corresponding transports are considered immediately for the deployment.

But enough with the preparation, let's have some fun with scheduling deployments! 😃

Use

Open the Deployment app to see all available deployment schedules. Use the filter options and create your own view if needed. You have the option to create or delete a deployment schedule in the table toolbar on the right hand side.

Note: Keeping an existing deployment schedule in Draft mode is not suspending the initially created deployment schedule.

Use Case #1 - Schedule deployment to QA every 15 minute

Create a new Deployment Schedule and do the following:

  • Enter a meaningful title
  • Select a deployment plan
  • Select one or several target tenants of system role Quality Assurance
  • Select the start time in the future (up to 5 minutes in the past it will be scheduled immediately)
  • Mark the "Repeat Schedule" checkbox and enter a frequency of 15 Minutes (minimum frequency 5 minutes)
  • Keep the "Release" and "Feature Status" (optional properties) empty to consider all features / transports assigned to the projects of the corresponding deployment plan 
  • Create the schedule

What happens now?

At 09:00 PM the first deployment will be triggered for all released transports with target O12.100 which are assigned to features of the deployment plan (in this case only assigned to one project). The import job on the managed tenant O12.000 is picking up the transports and imports them as an import subset (import all for the selected transports). This procedure will be repeated every 15 minutes.

Note: The frequency of the import use case on the managed system must be higher than the one configured in the Deployment Scheduler on SAP Cloud ALM.

Use Case #2 - Schedule single deployment to production for a certain release

Create a new Deployment Schedule and do the following:

  • Enter a meaningful title
  • Select a deployment plan
  • Select one or several target tenants of system role Production
  • Select the start time in the future (up to 5 minutes in the past it will be scheduled immediately)
  • Do not mark the "Repeat Schedule" checkbox and enter a frequency of 15 Minutes (minimum frequency 5 minutes)
  • Select a "Release" and select the feature status" "Ready for Production"
  • Create the schedule

What happens now?

On February 27 at 09:00 PM all transports assigned to features of release 02 / 25 in status "Ready for Production" will be deployed to O13.100. The import job on the managed tenant O13.000 performs the actual import as an import subset. This means an Import All for the selected transport requests is performed.

If you are not using releases you can create a deployment schedule for features in status "Ready for Production" and repeat the schedule weekly or bi-weekly to continuously deploy to production.

Further use cases

In case you are working with transport target groups it is possible to decide to which target tenants (clients) a deployment should be triggered. This helps in case you want to ensure a delayed deployment to certain tenants e.g. in Quality Assurance.

Note: If you want to deploy transports to a development client you must assign it to the Quality Assurance system role in a system group.

Traceability

How do I figure out which transports / features have been imported?

We recommend to use the Transport Analysis app to keep track of frequent deployment activities like every 15 minutes to Quality Assurance. You can create your own views to easily figure out the transports imported lately.  The Feature Traceability is beneficial for single deployment schedules to Quality Assurance, Pre-production or Production. Here you can easily check whether the correct set of features is ready to be deployed to the next stage and you can keep track of the deployment results. For more information, see Feature Traceability and Transport Analysis. 

The import scheduled through the deployment scheduler is logged in the history of the affected features. The history entry is added as "System Action".

Since the import job on the managed systems performs the actual import you can check the job logs for job name /SDF/CALM_CDM_IMPORT_TRANSPORTS in the respective target tenant to figure out which set of transports was imported.

Wrap-Up

The Deployment Scheduler in SAP Cloud ALM offers a simple option to automate your deployments. Unlock the full potential of your deployment processes with the Deployment Scheduler in SAP Cloud ALM. Elevate your continuous feature delivery, refining your workflow to achieve unprecedented efficiency and speed.

Stay tuned for exciting enhancements, and we are looking forward to your valuable feedback.

entry.title: Highlights from SAP Cloud ALM LAC Team in the BTP Experience Event
entry.content:
In the last week, representatives from our ALM team, Luiz Almeida and Vitor Wolke, participated in the BTP Experience held at SAP Labs Latin America. The event was a significant opportunity to demonstrate the capabilities of SAP Cloud ALM in connection with the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

During their session, Luiz and Vitor showcased how SAP Cloud ALM provides a comprehensive set of features designed to efficiently manage and monitor applications, services, and integrations, while facilitating the implementation of the BTP ecosystem. This capability is crucial in supporting the development and monitoring of diverse applications.

The collaboration and dedication of our BTP and ALM colleagues were crucial in making the event a success. A heartfelt thank you goes out to everyone involved for their partnership and contributions.

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite - Monthly Update February
entry.content:
SAP Integration Suite is an integration platform-as-a-service (iPaaS) that enables the design, development, execution, governance, curation, analysis, and management of the full lifecycle of integration flows, APIs, and high-volume business events that connect on-premises and cloud-based processes, services, applications, and data across organizations. SAP Integration Suite is available on multiple cloud providers.

In this webcast, we will have two sections:

Product Updates, where we share updates on key capabilities and latest enhancements released for SAP Integration Suite.

Interact with Integration Black Belt: An opportunity to hear and interact with integration expert.

OR

#AskAnythingIntegration: Our panel of experts will answer any questions you have on integration.

Links:

Meeting Request for this month

Meeting Request for the whole year

entry.title: Coming Up: Change in Preparation Plan for C_S4CFI
entry.content:
Hey there, if you're thinking about getting certified in SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Financial Accounting, listen up! The current certification version (C_S4CFI_2408) is going to be retired on March 31st, 2025. But don't worry, a new version will be available from April 1st, 2025.

 

 

Please be aware that the current certification version (C_S4CFI_2408) will be retired on March 31st, 2025. A new version will be available from April 1st, 2025. Why is this important?

Updated certification versions are nothing unusual. However, the upcoming version of this certification will have an impact on your learning preparation.

If you plan to book your exam before April 1st, 2025 your preparation for this attempt is not affected by this change. Just follow the preparation plan that is recommended in the certification description.

Otherwise, if you plan to take the exam from April 1st or later, then please be prepared that the following learning journeys and courses will be certification relevant:

Designing the Record-to-Report Process in SAP S/4HANA

  • F1211 Detailing Accruals Management
  • F1212 Detailing Financial Statement Reporting

Configuring the Record-to-Report Process in SAP S/4HANA

  • F1251 Detailing Configuration of R2R Posting Processes

Using the Payables Management Process in SAP S/4HANA 

  • F1221 Detailing Down Payments Execution
  • F1222 Detailing the Configuration for the Payment Run
  • F1223 Detailing Process Automation in Accounts Payable

Operating the Receivables Management Process in SAP S/4HANA  

  • F1231 Detailing Special G/L Transactions in Accounts Receivable
  • F1233 Detailing SAP Cash Application and Processing Rules for Bank Statements
  • F1234 Detailing Dispute and Collections Management
  • F1235 Detailing Credit Management Processes in Accounts Receivable
  • F1236 Detailing the Dunning Run

Designing the Asset Accounting Process in SAP S/4HANA

  • F1241 Detailing Settings for Asset Accounting
  • F1242 Detailing Asset Accounting Integration Aspects
  • F1243 Detailing Asset Accounting Year-end closing activities

In addition to this, let me give you a heads up about a change that's coming up. We're going to be making some technical changes to how we publish our courses, and because of that, your learning progress displayed on learning.sap.com might end up getting lost. So I just wanted to remind you to keep track of your own learning progress.

entry.title: How to create new Unit of Measure in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
Hello,

I have tried to add a new UoM in S/4HANA public cloud using the Custom Fields and Logic app and was not able to add an alternative unit of measure.

It seems like things have changed since the 2302 release and the Custom fields and logic app is now split in two.
When using the custom fields app I click Create, but there is no option for UoM.

How is this done in the 2502 release?

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to create new Unit of Measure in SAP S/4HANA Clou...
entry.content:
Hi there,

Please use the SSCUI 'Units of measurement' code 102179, under 'Basic Settings'.
After opening the SSCUI, click on 'Units of Measurement' and after on 'Create'.
There you will be able to configure the new unit of measure.

Kind Regards,
Ícaro
SAP Support

entry.title: SAP Integration Modernization Info Day - Essen
entry.content:
The world is changing faster than ever. Paradigms shift.

Driving innovation through integrated processes and data across the entire value chain - this empowers businesses to capitalize on advanced technologies, ensuring they can not only respond to change but also anticipate and prepare for it. Integration serves as a key lever that unlocks innovation and business expansion by leveraging the power and insights of your business data.

SAP suggests closing the rebate agreements in ECC and start fresh condition contracts in S4. In reality, there will be great push back from the business. Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Charging account assignment for PO with SES
entry.content:
Hi @MokraneAbdessalam,
If PO Item has PO history, then system will not allow to edit the Item level account assignment tab details.
If Invoice is not cleared for payment, we may reverse the Invoices and SESs, then create a new line Item in the PO for the same requirement with correct account assignment and then delete the existing line Item.
If the reversal is not possible, short close the Item by removing open quantity and Create a new line item with remaining open quantity  with correct account assignment.

entry.title: Structures et Domaines du Contrôle de Gestion sous SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Le Contrôle de Gestion (CO) dans SAP S/4HANA est bien plus qu'un simple outil de reporting. C'est une structure complète qui permet de piloter l'entreprise à tous les niveaux. Cet article explore les fondements du Contrôle de Gestion SAP CO, ses domaines clés et comment il répond aux besoins des utilisateurs.

1. Objectifs et Bénéfices du Contrôle de Gestion sous SAP S/4HANA :

Gestion Améliorée : Fournir une base solide pour la gestion stratégique et opérationnelle de l'entreprise.
Optimisation du Développement : Favoriser la croissance et l'amélioration continue dans tous les domaines de l'entreprise en :
Fixant et atteignant des objectifs clairs et mesurables.
Améliorant le résultat global de l'entreprise.
Assurant une réponse efficace aux besoins du marché.
Répondre aux Attentes des Utilisateurs : Le Contrôle de Gestion SAP CO est conçu pour fournir :
Des données réelles et comprimées : Informations fiables et synthétiques pour une prise de décision rapide.
Des données indispensables à la décision : Concentration sur les indicateurs clés de performance (KPIs) pertinents.
Une représentation claire et compréhensible : Reporting intuitif et accessible à tous les niveaux de l'organisation.

2. Les Domaines Clés du Contrôle de Gestion SAP CO et leurs Axes d'Analyse :

SAP CO s'articule autour de plusieurs domaines interdépendants, chacun offrant des axes d'analyse spécifiques pour un pilotage précis :

Contrôle Budgétaire :
Domaines d'analyse : Centres de coûts, Ordres internes, Unités d'organisation.
Caractéristiques : Planification budgétaire détaillée, comparaison Budget vs. Réel, analyse des écarts pour un pilotage proactif.
Calcul du Coût de Revient (CO-PC) :
Domaines d'analyse : Produits, Groupes de produits, Niveaux de nomenclature, Ordres de Fabrication (OF).
Caractéristiques : Calcul du coût théorique et réel, analyse des écarts de coûts, agrégation des coûts par nomenclature, déchargement des coûts, gestion des gammes et outillages.
Analyse des Segments de Marché (CO-PA) :
Domaines d'analyse : Segments de marché (clients, régions, produits...), Centres de profit.
Caractéristiques : Analyse budgétaire et réelle par segment, identification des écarts, analyse de rentabilité par marché.
Comptes de Résultat par Nature et par Destination (CO-OM) :
Domaines d'analyse : Centres de profit, Autres dimensions analytiques (activités, fonctions...).
Caractéristiques : Suivi des encours et des écarts, analyse du coût de revient par nature comptable, définition de règles de calcul complexes, analyse de la marge brute, du volume de chiffre d'affaires et des ratios statistiques clés.

3. Structures et Méthodes du Contrôle de Gestion SAP CO :

Centres de Coûts : Analyse des coûts par département ou fonction.
Ordres Internes : Suivi des coûts pour des projets spécifiques ou des événements ponctuels.
Centres de Profit : Analyse de la rentabilité par domaine d'activité ou ligne de produits.
Processus (Activity-Based Costing - ABC) : Allocation des coûts indirects basée sur les activités.
Méthodes de Coûts (Standard, Période, etc.) : Différentes approches pour le calcul du coût de revient adaptées aux besoins spécifiques.

Conclusion : SAP CO, un Cadre Complet pour le Pilotage de la Performance

Le Contrôle de Gestion sous SAP S/4HANA offre un cadre structuré et intégré pour piloter la performance de l'entreprise. En comprenant ses domaines, ses axes d'analyse et ses méthodes, les utilisateurs SAP CO peuvent exploiter pleinement son potentiel pour une gestion éclairée et une prise de décision stratégique optimale.

👉Quels aspects du Contrôle de Gestion sous SAP CO utilisez-vous le plus dans votre quotidien ? Quelles sont vos questions sur ce sujet ? Partagez vos réflexions en commentaire !

#SAP #S4HANA #Controlling #CO #ContrôleDeGestion #SAPCO #SAPFICO #StructuresCO #DomainesCO #GestionEntreprise #PilotagePerformance #Finance #Comptabilité #BlogSAP #SAPCommunity #GuideSAP #UtilisateurSAP

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mickaelquesnot_gusapco-pr%C3%A9sentation-g%C3%A9n%C3%A9ralepptx-activity-7298973290440605696-_UIn?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAAAJPDL8BjpJe3thZB6t04k7Z2hRVHhboAec

entry.title: Optimisez Votre Contrôle de Gestion avec SAP S/4HANA : Focus sur les Structures Clés
entry.content:
Le Contrôle de Gestion sous SAP S/4HANA est un atout majeur pour les entreprises performantes. Pour en tirer le meilleur parti, il est essentiel de comprendre ses structures fondamentales et d'adopter les meilleures pratiques. Cet article vous guide à travers les éléments clés pour un Contrôle de Gestion SAP CO optimisé.

1. Structures Fondamentales du Contrôle de Gestion SAP CO :

Centres de Coûts : La base du Contrôle de Gestion. Utilisez-les pour :
Collecter les coûts par responsabilité (départements, services).
Analyser la consommation des ressources et identifier les axes d'amélioration.
Piloter l'efficacité opérationnelle de chaque entité.
Ordres Internes : Flexibles et puissants pour :
Suivre les coûts de projets, événements, ou tâches spécifiques.
Budgétiser et contrôler les dépenses pour des initiatives temporaires.
Analyser la rentabilité d'actions ponctuelles.
Centres de Profit : Indispensables pour une vision orientée résultats :
Analyser la rentabilité par domaine d'activité, ligne de produits ou région.
Piloter la performance des différents segments de marché.
Optimiser l'allocation des ressources vers les activités les plus rentables.
Segments de Marché (CO-PA) : Pour une analyse fine de la rentabilité commerciale :
Analyser les revenus et les coûts par client, produit, canal de distribution...
Identifier les segments de marché les plus performants.
Ajuster votre stratégie commerciale en fonction des données de rentabilité.

2. Meilleures Pratiques pour un Contrôle de Gestion SAP CO Efficace :

Définir Clairement les Objectifs du Contrôle de Gestion : Alignez votre système de Contrôle de Gestion sur la stratégie globale de l'entreprise. Quels KPIs sont essentiels pour piloter votre performance ?
Adopter une Approche Intégrée : Exploitez la puissance de l'intégration de SAP S/4HANA. Connectez les données du Contrôle de Gestion avec la Comptabilité Financière, la Logistique, les Ventes... pour une vision 360° de l'entreprise.
Mettre l'Accent sur les Données Réelles et Pertinentes : Concentrez-vous sur les données qui éclairent réellement la prise de décision. Privilégiez les informations synthétiques et les analyses d'écarts.
Personnaliser le Reporting : Adaptez vos états de reporting aux besoins spécifiques de chaque niveau de management. Offrez des tableaux de bord clairs et des analyses "drill-down" pour explorer les données en détail.
Former et Accompagner les Utilisateurs : La clé du succès réside dans l'adoption du système par les utilisateurs. Investissez dans la formation et l'accompagnement pour maximiser l'utilisation de SAP CO.

🚀 En appliquant ces structures clés et meilleures pratiques, transformez votre Contrôle de Gestion SAP CO en un véritable moteur de performance pour votre entreprise !

👉 Quelles sont vos meilleures astuces pour optimiser l'utilisation des structures du Contrôle de Gestion SAP CO ? Partagez vos conseils en commentaire !

entry.title: Re: How do we migrate Open Accruals into a Condition Cont...
entry.content:
Hello,

In addition to the solution proposed by Hari, there are few other ways to migrate open accruals:

1. Migrate historical invoices for the purposes of settlement management. This can be achieved by writing a custom load program using BAPI_BILLINGDOC_CREATEMULTIPLE. This BAPI posts invoices without preceding documents such as sales orders. Once the historical invoices are in from the legacy system, a delta accrual run will catch up to the accruals and post them in the system and against the contracts.

2. Manually - use transaction WB2R_DOC_ENTRY you can manually post delta accruals against a contract (option 3). However, posting a manual accrual against a contract on its own will not help if you have a delta accrual settlement running, because you will be missing the business volume behind it to substantiate this extra accrual. Therefore, you either have to bring in also historical sales posted in legacy, or increase the business volume on each contract using REBD.

3. Manually - use transaction WB2R_DOC_ENTRY you can manually post accruals against a contract (option 9). Here you will have 0 settlement value, but you can use condition type REA5 to post the accruals (accruals from settlement). Here you also need historical sales or increase of the REBD for each contract.

4. Automatically - write a custom load program based on BAPI WLF_REGU_CREATE_FROM_DATA, which is the BAPI behind transaction WB2R_DOC_ENTRY. This BAPI lets you create manual settlement documents programmatically. Here also you need additional adjustment to the business volume either by bringing in historical sales, or adjusting the business volume.

Posting open accruals initially is possible, the problem is that at time of an immediate delta accrual they will be reversed if no business volume is created to back up these accruals. If no delta accrual needs to be run and this opening balance should be reversed in any way in the next settlement, then you can use WB2R_DOC_ENTRY to post the accruals either manually or creating an upload program.

entry.title: Using DYNAMIC partitioning with HANA Platform / HANA Cloud and leverage NSE (disk) storage
entry.content:
Table - partitioning is always a deep physical work to get the best results of the hardware as memory and CPU. HANA typical does need this only with very large tables. The rule of thumb per partition is some 300.000.000 rows (300 Mio ). The limit is with 2 billion rows per partition and with max 16000 partitions per table. Now these partitions can be either COLUMN LOADABLE or PAGE LOADABLE. A column loadable table/partition is in memory (and stored on disk), a page loadable table/partition in store on disk and fetched into memory if needed. This concept is called HOT and WARM data. In HANA this is call NSE (Native Storage Extension)

As CAP or HDI developer yo are more interested in the tables structures and not so much on the physical aspects of storing or accessing the data - only it should be fast. And what if the HANA could understand itself to build a new partition and you can live with a simple rule on top.

Now this is exactly where we start this blog. There is not the "one size fits all" solution but I think you can see the pattern:

Assuming you are doing financials and you most like look at data that are in the current year, maybe in the same quarter of last year. That is a span of 15 month you want to have in memory. All others data (older) should be on disk = PAGE LOADABLE. And you have a volume that fits in one month a partition size.

So we have

  • 1 month interval 
  • 15 month distance ( the limit where you switch from COLUMN to PAGE LOADABLE
    This is one year and a quarter - typical time range for many financial reporting tasks 

Lets us look at the syntax graph:

RANGE VALUES are:

PARTITION OTHERS are:

Sample:

Table:

 

DO BEGIN
    DECLARE DROP INT;
    select count(*) into DROP from tables where table_name = 'PART_1';
    If DROP > 0 then
       DROP TABLE PART_1;
    END IF;
END;

CREATE COLUMN TABLE PART_1 
    (A INT NOT NULL, 
     B DATE NOT NULL)
PARTITION BY RANGE(MONTH(B)) (
PARTITION '197001' <= VALUES < '202012' PAGE LOADABLE,
PARTITION OTHERS DYNAMIC INTERVAL 1 MONTH DISTANCE 15 MONTH PAGE LOADABLE
);

 

 Now we have everything before 202012 (Dec. 2020) as PAGE LOADABLE and the latest 15 month as COLUMN (DEFAULT) LOADABLE.

Now you can fill the table with some data:

 

TRUNCATE TABLE PART_1;
INSERT INTO PART_1
    SELECT A.A, B.B FROM
        (SELECT ADD_MONTHS('2000-01-01', GENERATED_PERIOD_END) AS B 
            FROM SERIES_GENERATE_INTEGER(1,0,296)) B,
        (SELECT LPAD(GENERATED_PERIOD_END, 10, '0') AS A 
            FROM SERIES_GENERATE_INTEGER(1,0,100)) A,
        (SELECT GENERATED_PERIOD_END FROM SERIES_GENERATE_INTEGER(1,0,1000));
 
--  force dynamic processing (or wait ~5 minutes)
ALTER TABLE PART_1 ADD PARTITION FROM OTHERS;
ALTER TABLE PART_1 REFRESH DYNAMIC DISTANCE;

 

This will nearly generate 30 mio rows. 

296 months and some simple numbers. On my HANA this is a 6 sec job. Enough to play and see effects of the partitioning.

Look for the table:

 

SYS.TABLE_PARTITIONS
SYS.M_TABLE_PARTITIONS

 

To find some more meta data on the partitions.

or

Important advice:

The dynamic instruction is not returned by this function but shows you how the distribution is in this very moment:

Considerations:

  • The HDBTABLE function should work against the dynamic declaration and will not need to work with a shadow table copy to conduct changes. Please verify upfront before you try in production. The general advice here is anyhow to use HDBMIGRATIONTABLE construct. This will make sure you not run into a shadow table mechanism.
  • CAP allows a @SQL APPEND command. Again the recommendation is to use @CDS.persistance.journal as well to enforce the generation of a "HDBTABLEMIRGATION" artefact.
  • The RANGES, intervals and distances one needs are very business specific. It depends on the queries and business demands to find the right balance between these parameters. 
  • If you fill the partitions not from older to newer you should use the dynamic_direction parameter as well.
  • The number of partitions in the DYNAMIC clause are limited to since recently 3000. That will be a lot of months.
  • To get a better filling per partition, we experimented with the following:

 

DO BEGIN
    DECLARE DROP INT;
    select count(*) into DROP from tables where table_name = 'PART_2';
    If DROP > 0 then
        DROP TABLE PART_2;
    END IF;
END;

CREATE COLUMN TABLE PART_2 
    (A INT NOT NULL, 
     B DATE NOT NULL)
 PARTITION BY RANGE (B) 
  ( PARTITION OTHERS DYNAMIC THRESHOLD 100000  DISTANCE 15 MONTH PAGE LOADABLE )​

 

 

 

This will give a filling of 100000 row (300000000 = rule of thumb) and for the 15 month distance. Please double check if this is working for you. The upper rule should work for the most cases. IOT scenarios might need the this one or a daily range. At a certain volume you should consider to so massive data into the SQL on Files in HANA option.

  • The clear advantage of this approach is: data are still fully accessible with the CRUD  that is defined. All SQL or CAP ODATA access will work fully transparent. 

Help:

NSE

heterogenious partion HANA Cloud

entry.title: Re: Custom check box on ME59N selection screen
entry.content: Look at the Dynpro generated for the selection screen, you will see global variables for text fields, e.g. if your checkbox parameter name is SSS, the global variable for the text will be %_SSS_%_APP_%-TEXT. You may set any text you want. ASSIGN ('%_SSS_%_APP_%-TEXT') TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<text>). <text> = 'Your text'.

entry.title: Re: The Ultimate Guide to SAPUI5 Personalization Engine: Fixing Hidden Issues
entry.content:
Hi Preeti,

Yes, you can definitely apply this personalization engine to multiple tables within the same view. Let me explain you with an example that I worked on. I have an object page with multiple sections, each object page section containing a table wrapped inside a fragment. In this scenario, I needed to enable variant management and personalization for each table. 

 

To achieve this, ensure that your view structure supports the above mentioned setup, and most importantly, maintain stable IDs for all controls.

 

 

<Page id="idPage" title="{i18n>Title.Empty}" showHeader="false">
    <uxap:ObjectPageLayout id="idObjectPage" upperCaseAnchorBar="false" flexEnabled="true" enableLazyLoading="true" useIconTabBar="true" sectionChange="onSectionChange" showHeaderContent="true">
        <uxap:headerTitle>
            <uxap:ObjectPageDynamicHeaderTitle>
                <uxap:heading>
                    <svm:SmartVariantManagement id="idSVM" showExecuteOnSelection="true" />
                </uxap:heading>
            </uxap:ObjectPageDynamicHeaderTitle>
        </uxap:headerTitle>
        <uxap:headerContent>
            <fb:FilterBar>
                    <!-- ... -->
            </fb:FilterBar>
        </uxap:headerContent>
        <uxap:sections>
            <uxap:ObjectPageSection id="idSection1" title="Section 1" titleUppercase="false" visible="true">
                <uxap:subSections>
                    <uxap:ObjectPageSubSection id="idSubSection1" titleUppercase="false">
                        <uxap:blocks>
                            <l:VerticalLayout width="100%">
                                <l:content>
                                    <core:Fragment fragmentName="Section1Fragment" type="XML" />
                                </l:content>
                            </l:VerticalLayout>
                        </uxap:blocks>
                    </uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>
                </uxap:subSections>
            </uxap:ObjectPageSection>
            <uxap:ObjectPageSection id="idSection2" title="Section 2" titleUppercase="false" visible="true">
                <uxap:subSections>
                    <uxap:ObjectPageSubSection id="idSubSection2" titleUppercase="false">
                        <uxap:blocks>
                            <l:VerticalLayout width="100%">
                                <l:content>
                                    <core:Fragment fragmentName="Section2Fragment" type="XML" />
                                </l:content>
                            </l:VerticalLayout>
                        </uxap:blocks>
                    </uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>
                </uxap:subSections>
            </uxap:ObjectPageSection>
        </uxap:sections>
    </uxap:ObjectPageLayout>
</Page>

 

 

In my "onSectionChange" event, I would determine the current section and call a function to fetch all the relevant data for that specific table. Additionally, for each fragment, I would maintain a utility file to handle its respective functions.

 

 

onSectionChange : function(oEvent){
var selectedSection = oEvent.getSource().getAssociation("selectedSection");
            if (selectedSection.includes("idSection1")) {
                Section1Util.getSection1ListData(this);
            } else if (selectedSection.includes("idSection2")) {
                Section2Util.getSection2ListData(this);
            } 
        },

 

 

In my utility file function, I ensure that the event registration for the personalization engine is triggered only once, rather than every time "getSection1ListData" is invoked. To achieve this, I use a simple flag to control execution. Since this function is used multiple times to fetch table data, I modified my code accordingly to prevent redundant calls. You can adjust this approach based on your specific requirements, but the key idea is to ensure that the personalization function is executed only once.

 

 

getSection1ListData: function (oContext) {
  var oThis = this;
  //necessary code for table data loading
  //Register Personalization Engine
  if(this.isTableInitial){ // flag
    this._registerSection1ListP13n(this);
  }
}

 

 

The core challenge in managing different personalizations lies in this part. In the "_registerSection1ListP13n" function, you need to retrieve the associated table of that section while ensuring the proper maintenance of keys, paths, and labels. Since there isn’t a direct approach for this, you’ll need to adjust your logic and code to fit your specific requirements.

 

 

 

 

_registerSection1ListP13n: function() {
                var oThis = this;
                var oTable = oThis.getView().byId("Table1Id");
                var aColumns = [],
                    listColumns = 
                          oThis.getView().byId("Table1Id").getColumns();
                for (var x = 0; x < listColumns.length; x++) {
                    aColumns.push({
                        key: listColumns[x].getId().split("--")[2], 
/* Use the column ID as the key. In my case, the column ID was embedded within the table’s path, for example:
'application-abc-Display-component---MainPageId--idColumn1'.
Therefore, my key would be 'idColumn1'.
To extract the table column’s ID, I manipulated it accordingly. You can adjust this approach based on your specific implementation. */

                        path: oThis.setTableColumnPath (listColumns[x].getId().split("--")[2]), 
/* I used a separate fuction to set the column path based on the column */

                        label: listColumns[x].getHeader().getText()// this path would give me the column title
                    });                    
                }
                oThis.oMetadataHelper = new MetadataHelper(aColumns);    
                Engine.getInstance().register(oTable, {
                    helper: oThis.oMetadataHelper,
                    controller: {
                        Columns: new SelectionController({
                            targetAggregation: "columns",
                            control: oTable
                        }),
                        Sorter: new SortController({
                            control: oTable
                        }),
                        Groups: new GroupController({
                            control: oTable
                        }),
                        ColumnWidth: new ColumnWidthController({
                            control: oTable
                        })
                    }
                });
// flag to indicate that the function has been executed 
                oThis.isTableInitial = false; 
Engine.getInstance().attachStateChange(oThis.handleTable1StateChange.bind(oThis));
            },

 

 

The function that returns the path based on column ID,

 

 

setTableColumnPath:function(oColumn){
                var sPath = "";
                if(oColumn === "idColumn1"){
                    sPath = "column1Path";
                }else if(oColumn === " idColumn2"){
                    sPath = " column2Path ";
                }else if(oColumn === " idColumn3"){
                    sPath = " column3Path ";
                }else if(oColumn === " idColumn4"){
                    sPath = " column4Path ";
                }else if(oColumn === " idColumn5"){
                    sPath = " column5Path ";
                }
                return sPath;
            },

 

 

The attachStateChange function triggers all associated attached personalization table functions, regardless of the current tab or table. Therefore, when the "handleTable1StateChange" function is invoked, you need to retrieve the current attached control’s table, match it with the section’s table, and then apply the necessary changes accordingly.

 

 

handleTable1StateChange: function(oThis, oEvt) {
                var selectedTabId = oEvt.getParameter("control").getId();
//gives you the table's ID that the attachStateChange event is invoking
                if(selectedTabId.indexOf("Table1Id") !== -1){
                    var oTable = oThis.getView().byId("TableId1"); 
                    var oState = oEvt.getParameter("state");
                    
                    if (oState === undefined || (oState && Object.keys(oState).length === 0)) {
                        return;
                    }
                    //Update the columns per selection in the state
                    oThis.updateColumns(oState, oContext);
                    //Create Groupers & Sorters
                    var aGroups = oThis.createGroups(oState, oThis);
                    var aSorter = oThis.createSorters(oState, aGroups, oThis);
                    var aCells = oState.Columns.map(function(oColumnState) {
if(oColumnState.key === "idColumn1"){
                            return new ObjectIdentifier({
                                text : { parts:[{path: 'oDataModel>column1Path},{path: 'oDataModel>texts'}],formatter: oThis.formatter.formatText }
                            })
                        }else if(oColumnState.key === "idColumn2"){
                            return new ObjectIdentifier({
                                text: "{oDataModel>column2Path }"
                            });
                        }else if(oColumnState.key === "idColumn3"){
                            return new ObjectIdentifier({
                                text: "{oDataModel>column3Path }"
                            });
                        }else if(oColumnState.key === " idColumn4"){
                            return new ObjectIdentifier({
                                text: {path: 'oDataModel> column4Path,formatter: oThis.formatter.formatColumn }
                            });
                        }else if(oColumnState.key === "idColumn5"){
                            return new ObjectIdentifier({
                                text: "{oDataModel>column5Path}"
                            });
                        }
                            });
                        }                    
                    }.bind(oThis));
                    //rebind the table with the updated cell template
                    oTable.bindItems({
                        templateShareable: false,
                        path: 'oDataModel>/Table1_Data',
                        template: new ColumnListItem({
                            cells: aCells,
                            type:"Navigation",
                            press: [oThis.onPressEvent, oThis]
                        })
                    });
                    if (aGroups && aGroups[0]?.vGroup !== undefined) {
                        let oBinding = oTable.getBinding("items");
                        oBinding.sort(aGroups);
                    }
                }
            },

 

 

You need to manage the other events for the table such as for updating (updateColumns), sorting (createSorters), and grouping (createGroups) in a similar manner.

I hope this explanation provides more clarity to help you move forward. Glad I could assist! 😊

Regards

Imtiaz N

entry.title: Re: Data Transpose in SAP Datasphere using Union Operation
entry.content:
Another way is just use a join with SAP Time Month dimension and use a case statement for derive the value

Example with PMCO

Link GJAHR from PMCO with YEAR from standard VIEW TIME MONTH

Create a formula

Like this

CASE MONTH
WHEN '01' THEN WRT01
WHEN '02' THEN WRT02
WHEN '03' THEN WRT03
WHEN '04' THEN WRT04
WHEN '05' THEN WRT05
WHEN '06' THEN WRT06
WHEN '07' THEN WRT07
WHEN '08' THEN WRT08
WHEN '09' THEN WRT09
WHEN '10' THEN WRT10
WHEN '11' THEN WRT11
WHEN '12' THEN WRT12
ELSE 0
END

Now you have the result in realtime

Example 

IN

 

OUT

You can also creat a SQL View but we prefer to follow lowcode no code if possible, more easy for maintenance

Patrice

entry.title: Costs & Revenues getting mapped against separate WBS elements in Public Cloud Professional Services
entry.content:
Dear PS Experts,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Professional Services app "Plan Customer Projects", the WBS element ending with 0.1 gets created automatically with billing element indicator as superior to the expense WBS ending with 1.1, which is the work package assigned to the billing item in the Billing tab. (This superior relation is observed in the CDS view I_ENTERPRISEPROJECTELEMENT as well.)

However, when we assign the expense WBS elements ending with 1.1, 1.2, 1.3, etc. to expenditures, these costs do not flow up to the billing WBS elements ending with 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, etc. However, the project billing is done only for the billing WBS elements. As a result, in the app "Project Profitability", we do not get to see the Margin mapped against each work package, as shown in the screenshot below.

 

Even if I create PO line items with the billing WBS elements ending with 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, etc., the timesheet hours of the resources are mapped by default against the expense WBS elements ending with 1.1, 1.2, 1.3, etc.

Kindly inform me how I can correct this, thank you.

Regards,

Pratap.

entry.title: Create a Nested IF Else calculated key figure (KF) that incorporates following elements
entry.content:
If current period is current period-1, then use actuals. Otherwise use snapshot 1. if snapshot 1 is null then use snapshot 2. 

 
ACTUALIZEDKF1@MTHPRODLOCCUST= IF( "PERIODID3" < "$$PERIODID3CU$$" -1, "ACTUALSQTY@MTHPRODLOCCUST",IF(ISNULL("snapshot 1@MTHPRODLOCCUST"),"snapshot 2@MTHPRODLOCCUST","snapshot 1@MTHPRODLOCCUST"))

Is there any issue with the formula? It is working fine with some exceptions where I have 0 in snapshot 1. I kept the system as "Uneditable" and no Aggregation Mode
